content:
# Top 5 posts from r/MovieSuggestions day

## 1. Old Comedy Movies

**Score:** 21
**Body:** Hi everyone!

I have been looking and looking for a specific type of comedy movies.

It is not a genre from what I understand, but movies from a specific era perhaps? I need old comedy movie suggestions like Coming to America, Trading Places, and Uncle Buck. Please and thank you!

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Thanks for making me feel genuinely old…

*”Stripes”* (1981)

*”Ghostbusters“* (1984)

*”Caddyshack”* (1980)

*”Private Benjamin”* (1980)

*”9 to 5”* (1980)
  - I saw the title and thought, "Oh, like His Girl Friday, Bringing Up Baby, or A Night at the Opera?" Then OP busted out movies my ass watched in the theater or on cable growing up 💀💀💀
2. Great Outdoors 

Splash 

Beverly Hills Cop 

3 Men and a Baby 

Overboard 

Mr Mom
3. Pretty much any John Hughes movie from that era is gonna itch the scratch based on your examples
4. Planes, Trains, and Automobiles
5. Um...80s comedies?
6. Midnight Run is a must watch, great movie.
7. Johnny Dangerously
8. Let It Ride

My Blue Heaven

Married to the Mob

Top Secret!

Real Genius
9. The Man With One Red Shoe (1985, Tom Hanks)

## 2. Looking for a good cyberpunk-esque mega city hellscape type movie

**Score:** 21
**Body:** I don’t mind if it’s a thriller an action movie even a romantic comedy - I just love the setting, I’m not looking for;

\- Judge dredd (including Karl Urban)

\- Robocop

\- Minority report

\- Bladerunner

\- Equilibrium

\- The fifth element

\- Total recall

Just because I’ve watched a handful of these films since I was knee high - thanks for reading any suggestions appreciated!

### Top 8 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Ghost In The Shell

Upgrade

Strange Days
  - Strange Days is a classic undervalued gem of a movie.
2. Alita: Battle Angel (2019)

What Happened To Monday (2017)

Dark City (1998)
  - I've always loved all those but Dark City i love the most. I've shown it to so many friends that never heard of it.
  - I really like Alita Battle Angel, can't wait for the sequel(s).
3. Demolition Man
4. Johnny mnemonic
5. Mute with Paul Rudd
6. Akira.
7. Outland

## 3. Fun offensive trash like John Waters

**Score:** 22
**Body:** Something outrageous, hitting on a 12/10 every scene, even Waters at his most restrained like Serial Mom still manages to include a pussy filled potty mouthed prank call. 

### Top 9 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. The Greasy Strangler
  - You're a bullshit artist!
    - Hootie-tootie disco cutie!
2. Russ Meyers films are trashy fun. Try Beneath the Valley of the Ultra Vixens.
3. Troma. I think what you’re looking for is the Troma library. Recommended starting points: The Toxic Avenger, Class of Nuke ‘Em High, and Tromeo and Juliet (likely the most similar to John Waters)
4. The Thingy: Confessions of a Teenage Placenta aka The Miracle of Life  


A female bodybuilder (played by a man) gives birth at the gym, throws the baby away and raises the placenta as a Christian soldier.   


Crazy Murder  


Deranged homeless man shits a lot and murders people.  
5. If it doesn't need to be narrative cinema, any and all of the Jackass movies. Waters has praised them himself.
6. But I’m a Cheerleader? It may be more tame than you’re looking for, but it’s definitely campy and queer.
7. I assume you have seen Pink Flamingo
8. Jon Moritsugu - https://jonmoritsugu.com/

Nick Zedd Or Richard Kern?

Trash humpers by Harmony Korine (or any of his films if you haven't seen them)

## 4. Good movies that deal with time or reality

**Score:** 20
**Body:** Movies like everything everywhere all at once and interstellar is what I am looking for, I want a good movie that is mind bending or trippy to watch  and has you really thinking about it and even maybe change your views on life

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. 12 Monkeys
2. Not a movie but I feel the TV show Dark can be mind bending and trippy and may change the way you see time.
3. Looper
4. Primer
5. triangle
6. Coherence
7. Predestination
8. Mirage (2018)(Spanish)\
Synchronic (2019)\
Timecrimes (2017)(Spanish)\
Aporia (2023)\
Palm Springs (2020)\
Triangle (2009)\
The Infinite Man (2014)\
Source Code (2011)\
Caddo Lake (2024)\
Beyond the Infinite Two Minutes (2020)(Japanese)\
Triangle (2009)\
Things Will Be Different (2024)
9. Inception
10. Aniara

Ashes of Time (kind of

Currently watching On the Silver Globe which also kind of fit into your theme

## 5. Movies where the protagonist gradually learns the antagonist is evil

**Score:** 18
**Body:** Looking for examples of movies where the protagonist gradually or eventually learns the protagonist is evil. The audience can know that they are early, or not. For example, Creep; Burning; Strangers On A Train. 



Any genre, but horror is ideal. Can be funny, serious, whatever. Recent or old, doesn't matter. 



### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Unbreakable
2. Single White Female

The hand that rocks the cradle
3. Not horror, but Training Day
4. Usual Suspects (but not gradually; more like over dropping a cup of coffee)
5. The Good Son
6. Heretic might fit?
7. Arlington Road
8. The Devil's Advocate
9. The Devil’s Advocate
10. Angel Heart

# Top 5 posts from r/moviecritic day

## 1. Anyone else think One Battle After Another is being read way too seriously

**Score:** 585
**Body:** I’m honestly kind of confused by the political hype around this movie on Reddit.

Every time it comes up, people talk about it like it’s some bold, radical, leftist call to arms almost like a modern Godard film from his 1970s era. But when I actually watched it, that’s really not how it landed for me at all.

To me, it plays much more like an absurdist comedy about how disconnected and self satisfied a lot of “revolutionary” leftist posturing has become. The characters who are supposedly the most politically aware aren’t portrayed as effective or grounded. They’re impulsive, unserious, and often more focused on vibes, aesthetics, or personal validation than on doing anything meaningful. At times it almost feels closer to a comedy sketch than a manifesto.

What really sealed this reading for me is the ending. The movie literally wraps up with DiCaprio’s character choosing to stay home instead of going out because he now has an iPhone. That doesn’t feel heroic or inspirational. It feels like a punchline, a joke about how easily radical impulses collapse into comfort, convenience, and consumer tech.

I’m not saying the movie is anti left, or that it doesn’t critique the right too. It clearly does. I just don’t understand why so many people treat it as an earnest revolutionary statement rather than a satire that, at least in part, is aimed at modern leftism itself.

### Top 8 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. I think a lot of people are still stuck on the "GRITTY AND REALISTIC" aesthetic that was popular from \~2000-2015 and can't comprehend that a movie can be deliberately outlandish or whimsical
  - Or more so that a movie can be multiple things at once, even in the same instance.
    - On r/movies a while ago there was a thread about how comedy movies never win Oscars and when someone would bring up Anora, Parasite, EEAAO people kept saying they aren't "pure comedies" as if they can't comprehend that a movie that is well made and takes itself seriously can be a comedy
2. I'm guessing a lot of people have not read Thomas Pynchon. Just felt like parody of radicalism from either side with a really cool father/daughter at the core. Enjoyed it a lot. Take things less literally folks.
3. The movie is about obsolete revolutionaries being discarded by an authoritarian future that seems unbeatable— except through the power of parenting that paves the way for a better generation (Pat is a fuck up but he has raised an exceptional woman) and communal effort (Sensei and his Railroad Subterráneo). The comedy derives from Pat's burn-out antics but it also gives the viewer hope in the face of fascism and seemingly insurmountable odds, which resonates with most critics, who are generally part of the left-adjacent cultural commentariat that is constantly shouting that the world is on fire (not that it isn’t). I agree though that people are taking it way too seriously. Politically it is not that complex or extremist.
4. Honestly anything that is smart enough to skewer multiple political perspectives at once today is considered revolutionary. I agree; it reminded me of Inherent Vice just with a better story.
  - I think this movie and Eddington are basically a double feature.
5. The whole thing felt satirical to me. Both sides of this revolution or whatever came across pretty crappy.
6. I'll have to hit the reviews cuz I'm with you and didn't take it as any kind of serious manifesto, so I'm shocked anyone else did. Yes, it discusses incredibly serious issues but most of the characters are fools one way or another (yes even the daughter).
7. it was more sature for me. like a lower key dr strangelove. actually felt like a kubrick tribute at several points. really like the movie and watched it twice now

## 2. James Cameron is the first director in history to have four consecutive films grossing $1 billion at the global box office.

**Score:** 360
**Body:** • Titanic (1997)  
• Avatar (2009)  
• Avatar: The Way of Water (2022)  
• Avatar: Fire and Ash (2025)

### Top 7 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. It’s surprising that he had no movies between Titanic and Avatar.
2. This man knows how to make a three-hour long movie feel like two hours. He knows how to drive people to the movie theaters. He knows how to keep cinema alive.
  - Avatar 3 definitely felt like 3 hours
3. Why didnt they follow up that smashing success with Titianic 2?
  - Titanic: The Way of Water
    - Titanic 2: Ah shit, here we go again
4. Is James Cameron a financial bubble?
5. And this is why when he presented a script for the next Avatar they bought it immediately despite them all being the same movie. Critics can moan, but a track record like that for bringing in billions is incredible. Let the man do what he likes, it's making bank somehow!
6. And three of them are the same movie!
  - It's a theme park experience without actually having to walk around.

## 3. What are your favorite soundtracks from any film(s)?

**Score:** 178

### Top 10 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. I loved the Oh, Brother Where Art Thou soundtrack.   I even saw the concert tour.  And it won a Grammy
2. Guardians of The Galaxy (2014).

It's interesting that they made the soundtrack part of plot,  indirectly, because of the Walkman and the retro music.
3. Trainspotting. How has nobody said this?
4. Last of The Mohicans. I watched it in a theater at a mall and as soon as I exited the theater I walked straight to the music store and purchased the cd. Instead of listening to my usual music (hip hop) I was bumping this for a good while.
5. Boogie Nights has a killer soundtrack. Also, Magnolia.
6. Garden State lol
7. Blues Brothers
8. Romeo + Juliet so so so good. The Leo one
9. Forrest Gump

Stand By Me

Dazed and Confused

Office Space

O Brother, Where Art Thou?
10. The Crow

## 4. Might be a hot take but this movie shouldn’t have been a Joker story.

**Score:** 153
**Body:** To preface this I love this movie and I think it deserves all the praise it gets… but why is it the Joker? Besides some character and location names it’s barely a DC movie it honestly feels kinda forced on just to get more comic fans to see it.

### Top 6 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Joaquin phoenix basically said this was a movie on mental illness that was disguised as a joker movie so they could get the funding needed
  - I called it EXACTLY that before I read Joaquin had said that, a complete bait and switch. Because this character has below average mental abilities, every bit of chaos he ends up creating is by accident (like Mr Magoo). He is opposite of the Joker, and there is no mechanism by which this guy can become the foil to Batman. 

It’s not a terrible movie… it’s just completely mislabeled.
  - I worked with a guy who was schizophrenic and he did the same laugh.  He was eventuality fired and unsuccessful tried to cut off his own hand and only got half way.
  - Ha! If you've watched The Studio that's basically the Kool Aid movie they were pitching 😂
2. “The king of Taxi Driving” or “Taxi driver of comedy” would have been a better title
3. I think that unlike most superhero movies, which are adolescent power fantasies, this movie is what wish-fulfillment looks like to someone who is severely mentally ill.  That's what I love about it.  It only gets awkward whenever it tries to tack its story to Bruce Wayne/Batman.  The Joker can't be sympathetic and terrifyingly amoral at the same time, and while this movie sticks to the former, it's mostly because of the latter I went to the theater in the first place.  To be fair, I paid for a ticket 3 times, so--at least to me at the time--I certainly thought it was special.
4. Loved this film. I don’t understand the reddit hate for it.
  - I don’t hate it, I just think it’s a mediocre movie that is (very openly) being derivative of better movies.

It’s also the fact that the DC/Joker license on it has led to it being over-praised in my opinion. I know some people, including some critics, do think it’s great.
  - I think it’s great too, but I was shocked it led the Oscars that year with 11 nominations. I guess it was the controversial wild card they always go for.
5. Phoenix gave an all time performance, so so good

## 5. What's your favorite Tom Hanks' movies?

**Score:** 17
**Body:** Mine is the green mile and road to perdition. Tom Hanks is definitely my most favorite actor of all time. 

I've never seen a bad performance from him. 

### Top 9 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. The Burbs
  - I watched this recently and it still holds up. Amazing characters
    - Satan is good  
Satan is our pal
2. Saving Private Ryan
3. Road to Perdition
4. Bachelor Party
5. Catch me if you can.
6. Why is Joe V Volcano never in these lists?  It's criminal.
7. Money Pit.
8. Polar Express

# Top 5 posts from r/TrueFilm day

## 1. What’s Three Colours: Red ending timeline?

**Score:** 16
**Body:** I’ve been watching Kieślowski’s Three Colours trilogy and I’m particularly interested in the ending of Red.

In the final TV news report about the Channel ferry disaster, we see that among the seven survivors are characters from all three films, including Karol and Dominique from White. The news explicitly identifies Karol as a Polish businessman, which places the event after the end of White.

However, this raises a narrative problem:
in White, Karol and Dominique are divorced, reunite only during Karol’s staged funeral, and the film ends with Dominique in prison. There is no clear narrative moment in which they would realistically be travelling together across the Channel, nor any explanation of why they would be going to England.

This makes me wonder:
	•	Is the ferry disaster meant to be read purely symbolically rather than narratively?
	•	Is Kieślowski intentionally abandoning temporal and causal coherence in favour of a moral or poetic closure for the trilogy?
	•	In other words, should the presence of Karol and Dominique together be understood as a reconciliation, or simply as a visual gesture of survival and convergence? Is the ferry shipwreck also based on true events or purely fictional? 

I’d love to hear a more expert opinion about this. 


### Top 1 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. Going over the backstory/origin of the three colours movies each movie represent a colour of the French flag and the ideals they symbolize. Blue/independence, white/equality, red/brotherliness.  

The three movies are Kieslowski thoughts in allegorical form of the future European Union. The ferry “disaster” happens as the characters/ideals are departing France to reach the UK/the rest of Europe.  

Technically it could be chronological and assume Karol and Dominique are back together based on the ending of White: Dominique in jail in a foreign land finally being equals as it is represented in the last shots of the movie where Dom and Karol are communicating. Or it could be just a metaphor of the 3 ideals crashing and returning to France, they’re not dead, it didn’t go as planned but maybe there will be another chance?!?  

It’s been a white since a watched them… White is my favourite.  

Absolutely no expert…
  - White is my fav too even though I read it’s the worst one from a pure critical cinematic perspective

## 2. Would it be awkward to see Marty Supreme with your parents?

**Score:** 0
**Body:** Hi. Awkward 19 y/o here that really wants to see Marty Supreme. Parents want to see it too but I read about the paddle scenes (lol)… 

How explicit is it? Would it make for an awkward viewing with your parents? 

I watched Anora with them last year and they were rolling their eyes at how “vulgar” it was, and some of those scenes were definitely awkward to watch with them lol. By the end of the movie they warmed up to it a bit once the sex stuff kinda toned down and the drama really kicked in, but overall it probably wasn’t their fav… 😬 (I loved it though). 

Is Marty Supreme on that level of raunchiness? 

### Top 1 comments and their replies (up to depth 10)

1. It is vastly less “vulgar” than ANORA: so it will be easier watching. The opening has a ridiculous and funny ~~sex scene leading to a delightful and bizarre sperm race.~~ That will be weird. 

Then you have the butt paddle sequence that you’ve already mentioned, which is very minor in terms of screen time and impact.

That’s the “sex” on screen. Everything else is whacked out chaos. So if your parents don’t like that, they will be weird. 

The major problem for “parents” I think is that Marty is an anti hero. He is an ambitious but disposable, immoral and amoral person. He isn’t *good*, and there aren’t many “good” people in the film. They are all serving to some extent Marty’s ego, and the wild failures of his selfish views of the world. 

I know my parents would not like that at all, nor would my partner’s parents, who told me once that no movie with bad people at the center can be good. So take that in to consideration.
  - There’s definitely more sex on screen than those two moments lmao but yeah it’s slightly slightly less in your face than something like Anora (which to be fair, is literally about a sex worker). 

But yeah there is more than just those two moments.
  - There’s also some pussy eating.

---
